regions.us-ports: Today in Charleston, winds are light from the southwest at 4 knots with overcast skies and a 1% chance of rain, posing no impact to port operations or shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Charleston will see an increase in wind to 11 knots from the south-southwest with drizzle and a 16% rain chance, while nearby ports including Savannah and Jacksonville may experience similar light drizzle under southwesterly winds. By 48 hours out, winds shift to the west-northwest at 19 knots with drizzle and a 25% rain chance in Charleston, which may affect shore excursions and embarkation activities in Charleston and Norfolk. Beyond 48 hours, expect northeast winds at 12 knots with drizzle and a 39% rain chance in Charleston, which could affect port operations and excursions in Charleston, Savannah, and possibly Port Canaveral. All other US Ports from Baltimore to San Francisco should monitor localized forecasts but no significant rain or wind impacts are indicated for today through the next three days.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today winds are east at 4 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds shifting to southeast at 8 to 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and drizzle reported at a 2% rain chance, mainly impacting ports from Key West through Grand Cayman to Ocho Rios, though rain remains minimal. By day three, winds will increase from the south-southeast to 12 knots with overcast skies and no rain expected, which could slightly reduce visibility during operations in Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná. No marine wind warnings or operational restrictions are currently forecast for any ports in the Bahamas or Central Caribbean through this period. Passengers and crew should anticipate smooth port calls with only minor cloud cover changes and negligible precipitation through the next three days.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an elevated rain chance of 61% with drizzle and easterly winds at 12 knots is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten, where overcast skies and wet conditions are likely. Current conditions in San Juan show light southerly winds at 2 knots with a 33% rain chance and overcast skies, indicating improving visibility early but increasing moisture through the day. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, rain showers with a 76% probability and east winds around 9 knots will affect San Juan, St. Croix, and Antigua, resulting in wet decks and potential delays during embarkation and disembarkation. Beyond 48 hours, drizzle with a 27% rain chance and east winds increasing to 14 knots are forecast for San Juan and nearby ports, suggesting lighter precipitation but moderately breezy conditions that may slightly impact outdoor activities. All vessels and passengers should prepare for wet weather and maintain caution during shore excursions through this period.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, easterly-southeast winds at 7 knots and clear skies over Cozumel support smooth embarkation and disembarkation with excellent visibility and calm seas for shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly more southerly, increasing to 8-9 knots with partly cloudy skies and rain probabilities near 13-16 percent, maintaining favorable conditions for port operations in Cozumel and Costa Maya. By 72 hours, winds strengthen to 11 knots from the southeast with overcast skies and a 39 percent rain chance that may affect shore excursions and tendering in Roatan and Belize City. Crew should monitor conditions closely as the increasing cloud cover and higher rain probability could require operational adjustments beyond two days.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today in Aruba, easterly winds are steady at 15 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase to 17 knots from the east with drizzle reported and rain chances at 14% and 8%, respectively, which does not warrant operational concerns for Aruba, Curacao, or Bonaire. By day three, winds will strengthen slightly to 18 knots east with overcast skies and no rain expected, maintaining stable conditions for Cartagena as well. Passengers and crew should note that while drizzle is forecast in the short term, it remains below thresholds for impact on embarkation and disembarkation activities across all Southern Caribbean ports.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today winds are east at 8 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for port operations and shore excursions in Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect east winds increasing to 13 knots with a 6% chance of drizzle today impacting Barbados and nearby ports lightly, followed by east-southeast winds at 10 knots and overcast skies with a 6% rain chance tomorrow, which may affect visibility for embarkation and tendering at St. Lucia and Martinique. By 48 hours out, winds will shift back east at 10 knots with a 6% drizzle likelihood, keeping conditions generally stable for Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten. Beyond 48 hours, no significant changes in wind speed or rain probability are forecasted, so cruise operations across the Lesser Antilles should proceed with routine planning under mostly steady easterly winds and low rain chances.
regions.los-angeles: Today at Los Angeles and San Pedro ports, winds are light from the WNW at 4 knots with clear skies and zero percent rain chance, providing optimal conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to increase to 12 knots from the WSW with partly cloudy skies and a 1% rain chance, which will primarily affect operations at Long Beach and Marina del Rey with minimal impact. By day three, winds shift to SSE at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and again a 1% rain chance, maintaining smooth conditions for Catalina Island cruise operations. No marine wind warnings are in effect, so cruise vessel maneuvering and passenger transfers should proceed without weather-related restrictions through this period.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the east-northeast at 2 knots with overcast skies and zero percent rain chance, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos to proceed without weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west winds increasing to 12-13 knots with continued overcast conditions; this sustained wind shift will be felt most at Ensenada and Punta Banda but remains below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Rain chances rise slightly to 13 percent on Day 2, which does not warrant operational concerns for cruise activities at these ports. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to west-southwest at 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 3 percent rain chance, indicating improving visibility and minimal weather impact for cruise operations across all three ports. Passengers and crew should anticipate steady winds under 15 knots and no significant precipitation through this period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, conditions in the Cabo San Lucas region feature light northerly winds at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain probability, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions at Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz to proceed without weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect westerly winds increasing to 8 knots with overcast skies and no rain chance, which will affect all three ports by maintaining steady but manageable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation activities. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to the west-northwest at 11 knots with continued overcast skies and no precipitation expected, so cruise vessels should prepare for mild wind-driven sea states while operations remain uninterrupted. No marine wind warnings or rain impacts are forecast through this period, ensuring stable conditions for passenger safety and excursion scheduling.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the north at 2 knots with overcast skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata with no weather-related delays expected for shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift westerly at 9 knots today and then become southeasterly at 9 knots tomorrow, maintaining overcast skies and zero rain probability, so port conditions will remain stable across all three ports with continued favorable conditions for cruise vessel maneuvering and passenger activities ashore. Beyond 48 hours, winds are forecast to return westerly at 8 knots with persistent overcast skies and no rain chance, indicating no developing weather trends that will impact vessel scheduling or port operations through the next three days. Passengers and crew can anticipate consistent wind speeds below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, ensuring safe and uneventful marine operations throughout the Mazatlan cruise itinerary.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in the Puerto Vallarta region, winds are light from the north-northeast at 3 knots under an overcast sky with zero percent chance of rain, resulting in smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to the west-southwest around 10 knots with continued overcast skies today and partly cloudy conditions tomorrow, maintaining favorable conditions for port operations across all four ports. Specifically, Puerto Vallarta and Punta Mita will experience steady westerly winds near 10 knots on day two, supporting normal tendering and disembarkation activities. Beyond 48 hours, expect winds from the west increasing slightly to 11 knots with overcast skies returning, which should not impact vessel maneuvering or shore excursions in any of the ports. No rain is forecast at any time during this period, so operations can proceed without weather-related delays.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, easterly winds are steady at 6 knots with partly cloudy skies over Barcelona, providing smooth conditions for embarkation and shore excursions across ports including Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and Ibiza, with zero percent rain chance ensuring no weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will remain easterly at 9 to 7 knots with overcast skies and a low 5 to 23 percent rain probability, maintaining generally stable operations in Malaga, Cadiz, and Gibraltar though some cloud cover will limit sunshine. Beyond 48 hours, expect easter-northeast winds increasing to 14 knots with overcast skies and a 30 percent rain chance that may affect shore excursions and port activities in Lisbon and Gibraltar. Crew and passengers should prepare for slightly breezier conditions and reduced visibility due to cloudiness in these western Mediterranean ports. We will continue monitoring for any changes impacting safe maneuvering or passenger comfort.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, winds are light from the ENE at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain chance in Naples, allowing smooth embarkation and disembarkation with no rain impacts expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a northeast wind increasing to 11 knots and a 50% chance of rain may affect port operations and shore excursions specifically in Naples, Civitavecchia, and Genoa, where rain could reduce visibility and require adjustments. Meanwhile, Marseille, Nice, Monaco, Sardinia, Corsica, Split, Dubrovnik, and Venice should monitor conditions as winds shift but rain chances remain below 30%. Beyond 48 hours, northeast winds near 13 knots and overcast skies with no rain probability are forecast for Naples, indicating stable but cloudier conditions for cruise operations in the Central Mediterranean. Passengers and crew should prepare for moderate winds and localized rain in select ports tomorrow while expecting calmer, dry conditions today and after day two.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today in Athens/Piraeus, winds are light from the south-southwest at 1 knot with clear skies and zero chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southerly winds will increase to 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% rain chance today, while by day two, SSE winds at 6 knots and overcast conditions with a 5% rain probability will be seen in ports including Santorini, Mykonos, and Rhodes without rain-related operational concerns. Beyond 48 hours, northeast winds will strengthen to 9 knots accompanied by a 36% rain chance, which may affect shore excursions and port activities in Athens, Izmir, and Haifa. Crew and passengers should monitor evolving sea and sky conditions as these changes develop, especially in ports with increasing rain chances. Overall, port operations remain stable today with only cautious watch advised for the mid-May timeframe.
